question: Q-t3-k2-digest-and-null
conjecture: On the A5 ML-woven graph with strict kernel-canonical claim continuity (no lenient fallback), K2 changes the K1 score digest for >=1 query on BOTH datasets, and the continuity-destroying null (deterministic target_claim_id shuffle, seed 4242) collapses those changes (real minus null sum > 0). Simultaneously the strict re-audit either sustains R2's depth-2 thaw or exposes it as lenient-fallback overcounting.

self_audit_commitment.issue: R1/R2 walker hop-2 contains a lenient fallback (any outgoing arc when no claim-continuous arc exists) that is NOT kernel-canonical.
self_audit_commitment.commitment: strict and lenient depth-2 counts are both published; if strict_depth2_min = 0, R2's progressive metric verdict is treated as overcounted and a correction fan-out (R2 results doc + LakatoTree annotation + INDEX) is executed regardless of T3's own outcome.

kill_conditions: primary = 0 -> depth-2 walks exist structurally but never move scores; walking lane dies on this substrate, R3 only.
novel <= 0 -> digest changes are not carried by claim continuity (artifact); mechanism claim dies.
Any gold consumption or new model/network/embedding call -> void.

locked_parameters.arm: A5 ML-woven; alias+soft(quality>0 floor)+hippo seed fixed
locked_parameters.continuity: strict for scores; lenient counted for diagnostics only
